findings.evidence: Grep: `UniformEllipticity.lean:97` proves `(c • (1 : Matrix n n ℝ)).toQuadraticForm' ξ = ...`; Mathlib defines `Matrix.toQuadraticForm'` via the matrix-linear `Matrix.toLinearMap₂'` in `QuadraticForm/Basic.lean:1203`.
findings.fix: Add the general lemma `@[simp] lemma toQuadraticForm'_smul (c : ℝ) (A : Matrix n n ℝ) (ξ : EuclideanSpace ℝ n) : (c • A).toQuadraticForm' ξ = c * A.toQuadraticForm' ξ` and derive the `smul_one` lemma from it plus `toQuadraticForm'_one`.
findings.issue: `toQuadraticForm'_smul_one` proves only the identity-matrix instance of scalar linearity of `Matrix.toQuadraticForm'`.

findings.evidence: Grep: `UniformEllipticity.lean:104` proves only `c • 1`; `matrixBilinearForm` is built from `A.toBilin'`, and Mathlib exposes `Matrix.toBilin' : Matrix n n R₁ ≃ₗ[R₁] BilinForm R₁ (n → R₁)` in `Matrix/BilinearForm.lean:98`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp] lemma matrixBilinearForm_smul_apply (c : ℝ) (A : Matrix n n ℝ) (η ξ : EuclideanSpace ℝ n) : matrixBilinearForm (c • A) η ξ = c * matrixBilinearForm A η ξ` and derive `matrixBilinearForm_smul_one_apply` from it and `matrixBilinearForm_one_apply`.
findings.issue: `matrixBilinearForm_smul_one_apply` is the identity-matrix special case of scalar linearity in the coefficient matrix.

summary: The new scalar identity formulas are under-generalized relative to the matrix API they extend; prove the scalar-action formulas for arbitrary coefficient matrices first and derive the `1` cases from them.
